questions.question_text: What values will be logged by the four setTimeout callbacks in the order they execute?
questions.explanation: The callbacks close over the variable i, which after the loop finishes has the value 4, so each callback logs 4. The other options either assume the value at creation time or a different ordering.

questions.question_text: What will be printed by the console.log(result) in the snippet below?
questions.explanation: Promise.all resolves when all promises settle, returning an array of their resolved values; both async calls resolve to 'data', so the array contains two 'data' strings. The other options either represent the raw Promise objects or an incomplete array.

questions.question_text: Given the following code, which order will the console.log statements appear?
questions.explanation: Synchronous code runs first, printing 0, then the microtasks from the first .then execute, printing 1, then the second .then prints 2. The other sequences incorrectly place asynchronous callbacks before synchronous output or reorder them.

questions.question_text: After the IIFE runs, what is the value of the outer variable x?
questions.explanation: The IIFE's var x is scoped to the function and does not leak to the outer scope, so the outer x remains undefined. Option 0 incorrectly assumes leakage, option 2 would throw, and option 3 is unrelated.

questions.question_text: Which value will be printed by console.log(result) in the code below?
questions.explanation: Promise.race resolves as soon as any promise settles; Promise.resolve('A') settles immediately, so it wins the race and result becomes 'A'. The other options ignore the immediate resolution of the first promise.
